item.title: In Love with the Way: Images of Path and of Self
item.description:
Rob Burbea explores how practitioners image the path, awakening, and themselves in relation to tradition. He contrasts the usual medical-patient and religious fantasies of practice with the scientific researcher and artist fantasies, then opens the question of what is most authentic, fertile, and soulmaking. The talk moves toward a gentler inquiry: noticing where fantasy is alive, where it collapses into ego-measurement, and how different areas of practice may call for different ways of engaging.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the inquiry 02:37 Eros and path fantasy 09:57 Medical model of practice 17:12 Bones need flesh 25:04 Researcher fantasy 37:59 Artist fantasy 47:50 Permission and scope 01:07:17 Five areas of practice 01:28:14 Open-ended path
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40178/

item.title: 'The Holy Life' (Part 3)
item.description:
Rob Burbea explores eros for the path: what it means to be genuinely drawn to practice, and how that desire shapes the way awakening is imagined. He contrasts a medical model of Dharma with scientistic, religious, and artistic models, then turns toward soulmaking as a more open-ended and personally fertile way to understand practice. The talk gradually widens from critique of attainment to a fuller account of beauty, mystery, and the movement between transcendence and immanence.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:01 Eros and the path 04:07 The medical model 08:31 Tradition and replication 12:00 The artistic possibility 13:38 Open-ended practice 22:30 What the soul wants 34:45 Outgrowing fixed images 45:04 Immanence and transcendence 58:17 Icons, not idols
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40189/

item.title: 'The Holy Life' (Part 2)
item.description:
In this talk, Rob Burbea continues exploring eros in relation to the path, asking what kinds of awakening can actually support desire, imagination, and soulmaking. He contrasts deflationary models of practice with more expansive ones, moving through papancha, emptiness, Vajrayana imagery, and the way different views shape what feels possible on the path. The talk gradually widens from a Buddhist analysis of sense experience into a broader inquiry into psychology, culture, and the limits people place on eros.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the question 05:05 Pleasure and reduction 11:15 Papancha reconsidered 17:34 Cooking and devotion 22:49 Vajrayana and emptiness 29:40 Eros in the path 47:43 History and deflation 01:02:23 Fear and limitation 01:17:33 A larger path
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40188/

item.title: 'The Holy Life' (Part 1)
item.description:
(Gaia House) PLEASE NOTE: This series of talks is intended for experienced practitioners who have already developed some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. In particular, it is strongly recommended that before approaching this set you study and work with the material from the following talks and series: The Theatre of Selves (Parts 1 - 3); Approaching the Dharma, Part 1 (Unbinding the World), and Part 2 (Liberating Ways of Looking); the three-part series Questioning Awakening, Buddhism Beyond Modernism, In Praise of Restlessness; Image, Mythos, Dharma (Parts 1 - 3); An Ecology of Love (Parts 1 - 4); The Path of the Imaginal (Longer Course); and Re-enchanting the Cosmos: The Poetry of Perception. Integrating that previous material and also taking the talks in this new set in their intended order will, for most, support a better and fuller understanding of the teachings from this course.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40187/

Integrating that previous material and also taking the talks in this new set in their intended order will, for most, support a better and fuller understanding of the teachings from this course. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Doubt and Discernment (Part 2)
item.description:
Rob Burbea continues a close examination of doubt, asking how it can become a workable instrument of discernment rather than a source of paralysis. He contrasts eros and craving, then extends the same kind of discrimination to rest, metta, imaginal practice, and jhana, showing how each can be present in ways that are either soulmaking or merely comforting. The talk keeps returning to emptiness and dependent origination as a basis for letting images, longing, and practice be taken seriously without being reified.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:01 Practice as preparation 02:07 Eros or craving 05:46 Wise effort 09:58 Questioning and experiment 14:08 Imaginal autonomy 19:09 Not realism, but relation 32:27 Emptiness as basis 47:05 Rest and jhana 01:01:37 Longing and devotion
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40169/

item.title: Doubt and Discernment (Part 1)
item.description:
Rob Burbea explores doubt around erotic imaginal practice by asking whether it is escapism, wish fulfillment, or a deeper way of meeting life. He turns those questions into direct inquiry, distinguishes eros from craving, and traces how imaginal practice can change engagement, perception, and the sense of the world. The talk moves from conceptual discernment to personal examples, then toward a wider view of the imaginal as relational, creative, and alive.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the doubt 03:30 Image asks for translation 06:44 Non-engagement or depth 09:10 Fleeing reality revisited 11:57 Eros versus craving 19:03 Fire, risk, and unbinding 25:59 Doubt, suffering, and path 32:45 Personal movement in practice 49:01 Living relationship and infinity
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40168/

item.title: Logos in the Garden of Souls (Part 3)
item.description:
Rob Burbea uses the analogy of musical hearing to ask whether the more imaginal dimensions of experience are “real,” or whether a better question is whether they are important and soul-making. From there he critiques flat cultural assumptions about science, realism, and mindfulness, and builds a case for a participatory view in which perception, mind, and world are not separate in the way modernity often assumes. The talk then widens into a careful sketch of soulmaking, Eros, and Logos, including implications for psychology, pathologies, developmental theory, and the limits of any conceptual framework.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Music and limited hearing 01:14 Real or soul-making 08:17 Culture and realism 18:15 Frameworks that support 26:02 Soulmaking and participation 34:47 Valid because valuable 46:41 Eros and development 59:27 Self, image, and pathology 01:10:33 Cracks in theory
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40181/

item.title: Logos in the Garden of Souls (Part 2)
item.description:
Rob Burbea explores how any Dharma framework also shapes what we can notice, value, and become. He argues that a useful logos must support soulmaking: imagination, eros, freedom, beauty, and the capacity for frameworks to break open rather than harden into dogma. In the second half, he turns to interpretation itself, questioning claims of neutral access to the Pali Canon and tracing how modern, scientific, and secular assumptions can quietly narrow Dharma and erase other ways of knowing.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 What a logos must hold 04:00 Eros and interpretation 09:07 When Dharma narrows 11:58 Whose Pali Canon? 14:58 Scholarship and tradition 20:26 Origins and dialogue 26:29 Epistemicide in Dharma 31:57 Mindfulness is not enough 40:18 Training ways of looking
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40180/

item.title: Logos in the Garden of Souls (Part 1)
item.description:
Rob Burbea explores the role of logos, or conceptual framework, in Dharma practice: not as abstract theory, but as something that shapes navigation, balance, and the very texture of experience. He moves from trellis and tree metaphors to emptiness, fabrication, and ways of looking, then widens into soulmaking, embodiment, relationship, death, ecology, divinity, and the limits of modernist views. The talk ends by asking for a framework that can include plural ways of knowing without flattening them into a single dogma.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:34 Logos as support 05:19 Trellis and tree 12:00 Concept beyond thought 19:12 Fabrication and emptiness 23:51 What the frame must hold 36:33 History and conditioning 39:14 Modern self and soulmaking 44:44 Holy bodies and Christ 51:19 Plural knowing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40179/

item.title: Wisdom, Art, Balance (Part 5)
item.description:
Rob Burbea continues a talk on balance in imaginal practice, focusing on how attention can become lopsided and how to restore fertility without suppressing experience. He distinguishes intensity from potency, then shows how sexual energy, erotic image, and the energy body can be worked with more skillfully. The talk closes by widening the map: shifts among desire, image, body, heart, head, and conceptual framework, and how a practice can be deliberately steered.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Balance in Practice 01:31 Heart and Image 04:31 Intensity and Potency 06:17 Sexual Energy 10:24 Union or Participation 15:55 Shifting the Emphasis 21:23 Degrees of Fabrication 23:16 Framework as Vessel
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40199/

item.title: Wisdom, Art, Balance (Part 4)
item.description:
Rob Burbea explores how desire can be worked with as part of imaginal practice rather than simply being suppressed or rejected. He moves from the question of balance into a series of examples: an unreachable beloved, a retreatant’s forest experience, and several ways of re-perceiving longing so that self, other, and world gain depth. The talk ends by contrasting an anatta reading of desire with ways of seeing eros as sacred, alive, and already participating in a larger divine or imaginal field.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:01 Eros and balance 02:16 The unreachable beloved 07:34 Beyond 'let go' 16:11 A transforming story 22:49 Include the self 28:13 Forest and benefactors 36:07 Love in the imaginal 47:06 A balanced constellation 01:04:49 Desire, divinity, and time
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40198/

item.title: Wisdom, Art, Balance (Part 3)
item.description:
Rob Burbea continues his discussion of mastery in practice by treating it as a fluid art of navigation rather than a fixed ideal. He moves through several ways of working with desire and craving: letting go, noticing vedanā and pressure, meeting underlying emotion with kindness, and skillfully redirecting toward nourishment or spaciousness. In the second half he turns to imaginal practice, looking at how to stay with erotic images, how to meet what feels hard to bear, and how shifting emphasis can open the practice without losing its tension.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Mastery as navigation 04:27 Why range matters 08:15 Letting desire pass 12:35 Vedanā and craving 17:26 Kindness for emotion 22:02 Subtle skill in practice 25:21 Skillful redirection 30:01 Opening desire's depth 40:35 Working with eros
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40197/

item.title: Wisdom, Art, Balance (Part 2)
item.description:
In this second part of the talk, Rob Burbea continues exploring how to build a vessel that can hold eros and imaginal practice without being overwhelmed by them. He emphasizes samadhi as nourishment and balance rather than just concentration, then broadens the frame to include rootedness, spaciousness, strength, and inquiry as supports for practice. The talk closes by turning to mandalas and paired images such as Akshobhya, Amitabha, Ratnasambhava, and Amoghasiddhi as ways of bringing emptiness, feeling, and action into a living balance.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Picking up the thread 01:07 Samadhi beyond focus 03:43 Nourishment and balance 07:01 Why understanding matters 11:21 Rootedness and earth 13:53 Spacious equanimity 16:58 Strength, libido, vitality 22:00 Dynamic balance 25:29 Range and inquiry
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40196/

item.title: Wisdom, Art, Balance (Part 1)
item.description:
Rob Burbea explores two alchemical sayings as a way of thinking about eros, practice, and the conditions that support a fuller path. He returns again and again to the need for a vessel that is strong and flexible enough to hold intensity, and to the question of how to work with fire without either repressing it or being overwhelmed by it. The talk then widens into preliminaries for eros practice: ethics, emotional and energetic skill, trust, and a more nuanced relation to equanimity, freedom, and duty.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Two alchemical maxims 01:54 Eros needs a vessel 04:41 Elasticity and shattering 08:56 Fire and its dangers 15:59 Dionysus and dismemberment 20:44 Preliminaries for practice 37:34 Ethics and rootedness 42:34 Energetics and trust 48:28 Equanimity and duty
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40195/

item.title: Eros Unfettered (Part 5 - Otherness and the Art of Disappearing)
item.description:
Rob Burbea continues his exploration of eros as part of soulmaking, contrasting it with older ideas of eros as a drive toward union. He argues that eros preserves otherness even while meditative practice can open real experiences of oneness, and then turns to the question of participation: how images, relationships, and Dharma itself become more alive when seen as co-created rather than fixed. The talk widens into questions of imaginal knowing, sacredness, and what it might mean for the Dharma to become endless rather than narrowly therapeutic or narrowly liberationist.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:01 Eros and otherness 02:02 Oneness and its limits 06:45 Meditative dissolution 10:52 Eros keeps tension 15:38 The birth of us 23:18 Not the art of disappearing 27:28 Participation and sacredness 40:59 Ways of knowing 57:05 Endless Dharma
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40175/

item.title: Eros Unfettered (Part 4)
item.description:
In this talk, Rob Burbea explores eros as a force that opens perception, interpretation, and soulmaking rather than simple desire. He moves from Plato and Kabbalistic images of infinite meaning into Buddhist language, then turns to the ways eros can be narrowed by literalism, greed, or reification. The talk closes with a careful account of containment: how boundaries and non-acting can become a temenos, a sacred vessel in which eros deepens into imaginal and spiritual creativity.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Eros Opens Meaning 02:32 Infinite Interpretation 07:04 Buddha Nature Language 09:38 When Eros Narrows 12:29 Dream of the Door 17:17 Relationships And Limits 24:10 Icon, Idol, Concept 29:14 Temenos And Containment 35:36 James On Desire
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40174/

item.title: Eros Unfettered (Part 3)
item.description:
(Gaia House) PLEASE NOTE: This series of talks is intended for experienced practitioners who have already developed some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. In particular, it is strongly recommended that before approaching this set you study and work with the material from the following talks and series: The Theatre of Selves (Parts 1 - 3); Approaching the Dharma, Part 1 (Unbinding the World), and Part 2 (Liberating Ways of Looking); the three-part series Questioning Awakening, Buddhism Beyond Modernism, In Praise of Restlessness; Image, Mythos, Dharma (Parts 1 - 3); An Ecology of Love (Parts 1 - 4); The Path of the Imaginal (Longer Course); and Re-enchanting the Cosmos: The Poetry of Perception. Integrating that previous material and also taking the talks in this new set in their intended order will, for most, support a better and fuller understanding of the teachings from this course.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/40173/

item.title: Dilemmas and Delineations: How did we get here?! (Part 3)
item.description:
(Gaia House) PLEASE NOTE: This series of talks is intended for experienced practitioners who have already developed some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. In particular, it is strongly recommended that before approaching this set you study and work with the material from the following talks and series: The Theatre of Selves (Parts 1- 3); Approaching the Dharma, Part 1 (Unbinding the World), and Part 2 (Liberating Ways of Looking); the three-part series Questioning Awakening, Buddhism Beyond Modernism, In Praise of Restlessness; Image, Mythos, Dharma (Parts 1 - 3); An Ecology of Love (Parts 1 - 4); The Path of the Imaginal (Longer Course); and Re-enchanting the Cosmos: The Poetry of Perception. Integrating that previous material and also taking the talks in this new set in their intended order will, for most, support a better and fuller understanding of the teachings from this course.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Tending the Flame
item.description:
(Gaia House) Please Note: This series of talks is from a retreat led by Rob Burbea and Catherine McGee for experienced practitioners. The requirements for participation included some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. Without this experience it is possible that the material and teachings from this retreat will be difficult to understand and confusing for some.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/37017/

The requirements for participation included some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. Without this experience it is possible that the material and teachings from this retreat will be difficult to understand and confusing for some.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/37024/

item.title: A Sacred Universe: Insight, Theophany, Cosmopoesis (Part 5)
item.description:
(Gaia House) The set of talks and meditations from this course outlines the foundations and some of the possibilities for opening up a practice of the imaginal. Please note that this set forms a progressively unfolding series of teachings, so the talks and practices will probably be more fully understood and absorbed if they are taken in order.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Ayya Santacitta, Ayya Santussika, David Loy, Thanissara: Mindfulness and Climate Action : 5
item.description: (Unknown) Thanissara with David Loy, Ayya Santussika Bhikkhuni and Ayya Santacitta Bhikkhuni. Unfortunately, Ven. Bhikkhu Bodhi was ill on the day of our call and was not able to join us. We wish him a speedy and thorough recovery. In this call, Ayya Santussika Bhikkhuni mentioned two resources: the international, interfaith effort Our Voices as well as the “Pledge to Mobilize” at the Climate Mobilization. She also invited those interested in taking action together as Buddhists on the national or local/regional level to email her at santussika at gmail.com. Finally, Dave Damm-Luhr mentioned the upcoming film from the same group that brought us “The Overview Effect.” This new film, “Planetary” (see the trailer here) maybe well-timed for action around the March deadline for targets into the UN climate talks that Lou mentioned. Location: Unknown Source: Dharma Seed Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/29723/

item.title: The Four Noble Truths of Responding to Climate Change
item.description:

Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Earth's Crisis - On the Edge of the Roof - (on climate change)
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Community of Washington DC) This talk views the ecological dis-ease of our planet through the lens of our evolutionary unfolding. We explore the egoic trance that has precipitated the destruction of our environment, and the inner practices of presence that enable us to respond from love and wisdom.

item.title: Climate Change, Capitalism, and Buddhadharma
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley) An impassioned talk that includes a little known story from the suttas about the dangers of and suffering caused by the notion of private property. During the talk, winds of 45 mph buffeted the monastery, emergency sirens sounded in the distance, and three power outages occurred. Ends with a lively discussion with the sangha.
Location: Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/21552/

item.title: Follow Your Heartbreak
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley) Inspired by Andrew Harvey's book A Guide to Sacred Activism. The journey of transformation, both personal and global, includes our hearts opening to all the suffering we encounter and letting our heart be shattered and break open to new possibilities. This is a natural and essential component of true awakening. This process is explored on different levels: our own dharma practice, the classical Progress of Insight and what Harvey calls "The Dark Night of the Species."
Location: Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/20497/
item.itunes:author: James Baraz

item.title: Connecting Inner and Outer Responses to Climate Change
item.description:
(Spirit Rock Meditation Center) At this time of climate disruption, we need powerful responses--integrating more "inner" spiritual practices and principles, on the one hand, with skill in "outer" responses, on the other. This integration or marriage can happen in many ways as we participate in the "great turning"--whether our primary emphasis, to use Joanna Macy's analysis, is stopping further damage from occurring, transforming our institutions, or helping to shift consciousness. Without this integration, however, spiritual practice runs the risk of becoming a kind of middle-class escapism and activism runs the risk of being caught in self-righteousness, attachment to views, demonization of the "enemy," and burnout. We need a new integration! We look at several dharma principles that can be the basis for such an integration, consider briefly how Spirit Rock is responding (and might respond further) to climate issues, and especially look at the figure of the bodhisattva.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/19240/
item.itunes:author: Donald Rothberg

item.title: Dharma practice and the climate Crisis
item.description:
(Spirit Rock Meditation Center) At the time of Earth Day, we pose a a dilemma and challenge. Dharma practice is so compelling. The climate crisis is so compelling. Which do we choose? Both, in our own ways. We look at the ways that our practice (and principles of generosity, compassion, ethics, interdependence, and wisdom) call us to regard and that those responding deeply need spiritual practices and principles to meet the great need.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/19116/
item.itunes:author: Donald Rothberg

item.title: Stewardship and Intention as Gaia Practices
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley) This is part 4 of the series 'Appropriate Response at the Tipping Point' that began 6/14. In this talk, James discusses the last two of the five transformational commitments in Bob Doppelt's book From Me to We:

4. Acknowledge your trustee obligations and take responsibility for the continuation of all life
5. Choose your own destiny

item.title: Karma and Sila as Gaia Practices
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley) This is part 3 of the series 'Appropriate Response at the Tipping Point' that began 6/14. In this talk, James discusses the first three of the five transformational commitments in Bob Doppelt's book From Me to We:

1. See the systems you are part of
2. Be accountable for all the consequences of your actions
3. Abide by society’s most deeply held universal principles of morality and justice

Location: Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley Source: Dharma Seed Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/16555/

item.title: Seeing the System You Are Part Of
item.description: (Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley) Sharing a positive vision for the future from Ernest Callenbach’s "Epistle to Ecotopians". We then begin to explore the first of five Dharma principles using systems and sustainability expert Bob Doppelt’s book From Me to We. The ignorance of these principles is what perpetuates the problem and the understanding of them is the key to changing our consciousness and providing a path toward healing the planet. This is part 2 of the series 'Appropriate Response at the Tipping Point' that began June 14. Location: Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley Source: Dharma Seed Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/16516/

item.title: Appropriate Response at the Tipping Point: Concern for the Planet as Dharma Practice
item.description: (Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley) This is the first of a four-part series inspired by the Spring 2012 issue of Inquiring Mind, entitled 'Earth Now.' In addition to reading this issue, especially the interview with Joanna Macy: Woman on the Edge of Time, James also suggested these readings: Ernest Callenbach’s essay "Epistle to Ecotopians" and Bob Doppelt's book From Me to We: The Five Transformational Commitments Required to Rescue the Planet, Your Organization, and Your Life. The series continues on 6/28, 7/5, and 7/19. Location: Insight Meditation Community of Berkeley Source: Dharma Seed Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/16313/

item.title: For the Sake of Life on Earth, part 2

item.title: For The Sake of Life on Earth, part 1
item.description: (Spirit Rock Meditation Center) Through silence and song, sitting practice. Dharma talks, and interactive exercises, we will affirm our capacity to take part in the healing of our world. Each of our lives is played against a backdrop of war-making, hunger, mass extinctions and increasing climate chaos. The Buddha's teachings show how we can respond to the suffering of our time in ways that bring courage, gladness, and deep community. Our day together will draw on the Work That Reconnects, developed by Joanna over the last 35 years, and on Jennifer's soul-expanding music, that has empowered activists the world over. Joanna Macy is known worldwide as a Gaian teacher whose trainings strengthen movements for global justice. Her books include Dharma and Development, Coming Back To Life, Mutual Causality in Buddhist Teachings and Systems Theory, and her memoir Widening Circles, as well as translations of Rilke's poetry. Jennifer Berezan is a renowned singer/songwriter whose work is informed by Buddhist and indigenous teachings as well as her adventures at the forefront of social change. Her concerts and recordings include Praises for the World and her most recent release In These Arms, a Song for All Beings, based on practices of loving kindness and compassion. Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center Source: Dharma Seed Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/13571/

item.description:

item.title: The Self and Its Search for Security
item.description:
(Gaia House) This talk investigates some of the ways the ego mistakenly seeks security in different places, particularly exploring our complex relationship with money, and with self-view, and then also inquiring into the very structure of that self.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Blessing Life - The Power of the Generous Heart
item.description:
(Gaia House) Our capacity for generosity is one of the most beautiful of human capacities. This talk explores some of the profound and far-reaching possibilities of the practice of generosity - its power to open our hearts wide and to effect a deep and radical transformation in our lives.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9810/

item.title: Opening the Sense of What's Possible
item.description:
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9816/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Realizing the Nature of Mind
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge) Through practice we can glimpse a sense of the nature of awareness as something ever present and awesomely vast, and this sense can be cultivated as a profound resource for freedom and peace in our lives. But eventually we must see even beyond this to know the ultimate nature of the mind - empty, completely groundless, and dependently-arisen - a seeing which brings an even deeper freedom. This talk explores some of the ways this realization might be encouraged and developed in meditation.
Location: Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/6349/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge) Through practice we can glimpse a sense of the nature of awareness as something ever present and awesomely vast, and this sense can be cultivated as a profound resource for freedom and peace in our lives. But eventually we must see even beyond this to know the ultimate nature of the mind - empty, completely groundless, and dependently-arisen - a seeing which brings an even deeper freedom. This talk explores some of the ways this realization might be encouraged and developed in meditation. Location: Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: No Self, No World
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge) Anatta (‘not-self’, or ‘no-self’) is one of the Buddha’s most subtle and profound teachings, and a full understanding of it involves seeing how not only the personal self, but also the entire world of experience, is empty of any intrinsic essence or existence. This talk explores some of the possible ways a meditator might work in practice to develop and strengthen such radical and liberating insights.
Location: Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/6300/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge) Anatta (‘not-self’, or ‘no-self’) is one of the Buddha’s most subtle and profound teachings, and a full understanding of it involves seeing how not only the personal self, but also the entire world of experience, is empty of any intrinsic essence or existence. This talk explores some of the possible ways a meditator might work in practice to develop and strengthen such radical and liberating insights. Location: Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Into the depths of Silence
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge) Listening to silence in our lives, opening to its embrace, reveals a profound and immense power to transform the heart. Deepening in the stillness of meditation, our practice involves mindfulness of all ‘objects’, but must eventually also go beyond objects to realize a truly boundless freedom – of being nothing and having nothing.
Location: Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/6275/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge) Listening to silence in our lives, opening to its embrace, reveals a profound and immense power to transform the heart. Deepening in the stillness of meditation, our practice involves mindfulness of all ‘objects’, but must eventually also go beyond objects to realize a truly boundless freedom – of being nothing and having nothing. Location: Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: What is ‘Insight’?
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge) This talk presents a map and overview of the path of Insight Meditation, exploring the different kinds and levels of insight available to us, the possible avenues for its development and deepening, and some of the many ways we can nurture and strengthen its unfolding.
Location: Insight Meditation Society - Forest Refuge
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/6254/

item.title: Maya and Nirvana (Beyond the Measure of Mind)
item.description:
(Gaia House) What did the Buddha mean when he said that the world is 'like an illusion or a magic show' and that 'only nirvana is real'? This talk explores some of the Buddha's teaching of Dependent Arising, and how, through right practice, the illusion may be untangled, revealing the true nature of things, of mind, and of Nirvana.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/10832/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) What did the Buddha mean when he said that the world is 'like an illusion or a magic show' and that 'only nirvana is real'? This talk explores some of the Buddha's teaching of Dependent Arising, and how, through right practice, the illusion may be untangled, revealing the true nature of things, of mind, and of Nirvana. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Living in the Truth
item.description:
(Gaia House) A life dedicated to discovering the Truth of things is a life lived beautifully, and leading organically to freedom from suffering. Yet we frequently approach our seeking with hidden attachments to assumptions, preconceptions and views (often about Truth or the ways it is realised) that hinder a really complete, far-reaching, open and radical inquiry. On every level, from the personal to the mystical and ultimate, how can we give free reign to the heart's longing to live the truth?
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/10831/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) A life dedicated to discovering the Truth of things is a life lived beautifully, and leading organically to freedom from suffering. Yet we frequently approach our seeking with hidden attachments to assumptions, preconceptions and views (often about Truth or the ways it is realised) that hinder a really complete, far-reaching, open and radical inquiry. On every level, from the personal to the mystical and ultimate, how can we give free reign to the heart's longing to live the truth? Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Sense Desire
item.description:
(Gaia House) We exist and move in the world of sense contacts, and yet often we neglect to examine this relationship very deeply, or it becomes just another way to judge ourselves. Can we challenge our assumptions, habits and views and inquire caringly in this area in order to open to a more profound and unexpected freedom?
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/10833/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) We exist and move in the world of sense contacts, and yet often we neglect to examine this relationship very deeply, or it becomes just another way to judge ourselves. Can we challenge our assumptions, habits and views and inquire caringly in this area in order to open to a more profound and unexpected freedom? Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Working with Thought in Meditation
item.description:
(Gaia House) As meditators, it can be tempting to see the thinking mind as an enemy, but we can instead discover a wise and skillful relationship with it. This talk presents possible ways of working with the wide range of manifestations of thought: from papanca (proliferation) and obsession to creative, reflective and helpful thinking, to seeing the empty nature of all thought.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/10835/

item.title: Talk Five: Samatha, Nibbana, and the Emptiness of Perception - The Relationship Between Concentration and Insight
item.description:
(Gaia House) As we learn to develop concentration in meditation, samatha (calm, tranquility) is also developed, and together these qualities become a powerful means for deep insight and a source of profound well-being. This progressive series of talks, guided meditations and instructions explores in some detail the art of concentration, primarily through different ways of working with the breath and the body to open to deeper and deeper levels of calmness, presence and joy.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11513/

item.title: Nirvana and Transcendence
item.description:
(Gaia House) Is this it? Or has transcendence gone out of fashion? An exploration of the Buddha's radical teachings on transcendence and their implications for our practices and for our lives.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11974/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) Is this it? Or has transcendence gone out of fashion? An exploration of the Buddha's radical teachings on transcendence and their implications for our practices and for our lives. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Awakening
item.description:
(Gaia House) The Realization of Awakening is absolutely central to the Buddha's Teaching, and yet it has come to have so many different meanings, or none at all. What is our relationship to the idea of Enlightenment or Awakening? How does that affect our life and practice? Can we explore the views, assumptions and emotions surrounding it? And is a radical Awakening possible for us?
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11862/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) The Realization of Awakening is absolutely central to the Buddha's Teaching, and yet it has come to have so many different meanings, or none at all. What is our relationship to the idea of Enlightenment or Awakening? How does that affect our life and practice? Can we explore the views, assumptions and emotions surrounding it? And is a radical Awakening possible for us? Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Love/The Difficulties of Love
item.description:
(Gaia House) This talk begins to explore some of the challenges and difficulties we can sometimes encounter with regard to love. It also examines some possible beliefs and attitudes we may hold around love and particularly romantic love in our lives.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11860/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) This talk begins to explore some of the challenges and difficulties we can sometimes encounter with regard to love. It also examines some possible beliefs and attitudes we may hold around love and particularly romantic love in our lives. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Death
item.description:
(Gaia House) A free and honest life includes the very real awareness of death. If we can find the courage to deliberately contemplate death, to keep it in mind, this can open our life in a profound way to a nobility, urgency, purposefulness and beauty. The heart grows in compassion and moves toward the Deathless.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11859/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) A free and honest life includes the very real awareness of death. If we can find the courage to deliberately contemplate death, to keep it in mind, this can open our life in a profound way to a nobility, urgency, purposefulness and beauty. The heart grows in compassion and moves toward the Deathless. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Opening Talk for Work Retreat - Working and Awakening
item.description:
(Gaia House) Opening talk for a retreat that looks at how we may create artificial divisions between what seems 'sacred' and what seems 'worldly'; between 'retreat life' and 'everyday life', 'meditation' and work' and so seek transformation through only a small part of our lives. In opening to a profound wisdom that sees beyond such dualities, we can come to know an authentic freedom that is truly boundless.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11861/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea

In opening to a profound wisdom that sees beyond such dualities, we can come to know an authentic freedom that is truly boundless. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: The Birth of a Bodhisattva
item.description:
(Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11954/

item.title: Joy
item.description:
(Gaia House) The Buddha placed great emphasis on joy (mudita) and Happiness (sukkha) as part of the path. This talk explores the place of these beautiful and precious qualities in our practice, and life. What are the foundations of happiness? What is it that allows the heart to open to joy?
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11971/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) The Buddha placed great emphasis on joy (mudita) and Happiness (sukkha) as part of the path. This talk explores the place of these beautiful and precious qualities in our practice, and life. What are the foundations of happiness? What is it that allows the heart to open to joy? Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: What is Insight?
item.description:
(Gaia House) This talk presents a map and overview of the path of Insight Meditation, exploring the different kinds and levels of insight available to us, the possible avenues for its development and deepening, and some of the many ways we can nurture and strengthen its unfolding.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11972/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) This talk presents a map and overview of the path of Insight Meditation, exploring the different kinds and levels of insight available to us, the possible avenues for its development and deepening, and some of the many ways we can nurture and strengthen its unfolding. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Time and the Emptiness of Time
item.description:
(Gaia House) Time is one of the fundamental aspects of existence we usually take for granted, leaving its seeming ‘reality’ unquestioned. But Dharma practice and inquiry can puncture and shatter our belief in the real independent existence of time – both the past/future and the present/the ‘Now’, and, in so doing, open awareness up to a truly inconceivable and immeasurable liberation.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11929/

item.title: A Question of Faith
item.description:
(Gaia House) A probing exploration of the movements of faith in our practice. Whether we are conscious of it or not, we are always placing our faith somewhere. What do we have faith in? What shapes our sense of limitation or possibility? And how do faith and confidence deepen?
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11927/

item.title: Freedom From Fear And Anxiety
item.description:
(Insight Meditation Society - Retreat Center) This talk explores in detail the tools and approaches we can develop to work skillfully with fear in our lives, both those fears that are obvious and those that are more hidden. There is a genuine possibility of liberation from the power that fear seems to have to constrict us
Location: Insight Meditation Society - Retreat Center
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Passion, Desire, and the Path
item.description:
(Gaia House) Often in spiritual traditions desire is seen simply as something to rid ourselves of, but there is a very important place for it on the path. We need passion. And we need both to understand desire and to know freedom from desire.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12343/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea

item.title: Equanimity in Compassion
item.description:
(Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12289/

item.title: The Nature of Awareness
item.description:
(Gaia House) Realising the Nature of Mind, of Consciousness, of 'That Which Knows', brings a profound liberation to life. This talk explores an investigation into Awareness itself.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Renunciation and Joy
item.description:
(Gaia House) Renunciation is a central ingredient of the path to Awakening and is intimately connected with our heart's capacity for deep joy, wonder, openness and freedom. What might it mean for us in our lives?
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12860/
